---
title: "AI-Native Dissertation Framework"
subtitle: "A reproducible, forkable framework for writing dissertations with AI as a collaborator"
---

## Welcome

This is a demonstration of the **AI-native dissertation framework** — a system that unifies your dissertation text, statistical analyses, and interactive dissemination into a single reproducible repository.

The framework is built on three pillars:

1. **An R package** (`dissertation`) that houses your data, analysis functions, and REST API
2. **Quarto documents** (`.qmd`) that serve as both your dissertation chapters and website content
3. **A NextJS web application** (`dissertation-ai`) that provides interactive exploration, visualizations, and an AI chatbot

## How It Works

Your dissertation lives in two repositories:

- **`dissertation-rpkg`** (this repository): The R package containing data, analyses, documentation, and Quarto source files
- **`dissertation-ai`**: The NextJS application that wraps the R package in an interactive web experience

The same Quarto source files produce both a **thesis-ready PDF** (using your institution's formatting requirements) and an **interactive website** where readers can explore your methods, results, and code.

## Quick Start

```r
# Install the package
devtools::install(".")

# Load and explore the data
library(dissertation)
data(assessmentData)
summarizeAssessment(content_area = "READING", group_by = "GRADE")

# Start the REST API
run_dissertation_api(port = 8000)
```

## Dissertation Chapters

- [Chapter 1: Introduction](content/chapters/introduction.qmd)
- [Chapter 2: Literature Review](content/chapters/literature_review.qmd)
- [Chapter 3: Methods](content/chapters/methods.qmd)
- [Chapter 4: Results](content/chapters/results.qmd)
- [Chapter 5: Discussion](content/chapters/discussion.qmd)

## Start From Scratch with AI

If you use an AI coding assistant (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, or similar), you can skip forking entirely. Download the setup file, drop it into an empty directory, and let your AI agent build a fully customized dissertation framework around your research topic.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Download SETUP.md

[{{< iconify bi:download >}} **SETUP.md**](assets/SETUP.md){.btn .btn-primary download="SETUP.md"} — Complete instructions for any AI agent to scaffold your dissertation framework from scratch.

**How to use it:**

1. Create a new directory for your dissertation
2. Place `SETUP.md` inside it (along with any research notes, proposals, or data you have)
3. Open your AI agent and say: *"I'm ready to get started writing my dissertation. Read SETUP.md first."*

The agent will ask about your research topic, questions, data, and institution — then build everything customized to you.
:::

## For Training Participants

This framework was presented at a training session on writing AI-native dissertations. To use it for your own work:

1. Fork both repositories
2. Replace the seed dataset with your own data
3. Modify the analysis functions for your research questions
4. Write your dissertation chapters in the Quarto files
5. The web application and API automatically adapt

See the [Customization Guide](customization_guide.qmd) for detailed instructions.
